description: Fred Rocba home workstation — physical break-in and IP theft from Stark Research Labs employee system (FOR500 case)
scenario: Physical intrusion at employee home during vacation. Attacker accessed SRL laptop left logged in. Goal: steal research IP. Break-in date: 2020-11-13 evening EST.

iocs.description: Unauthorized access window while Fred was in Disney World
Door forced open — targeted physical intrusion focused on SRL laptop
Key projects Fred had access to were targeted — need to determine what was stolen
Attacker moved data off system — method and destination unknown
Attacker used Fred's active session — system was left logged in during vacation
Accessed corporate IP — biotech, metals research, advanced alloy R&D
Check for USB insertion events during break-in window (2020-11-13 evening)
Check browser history for cloud upload, file sharing, or tool downloads during attack window
Fred used RDP for work — check if attacker also leveraged existing RDP sessions or created new ones

investigation_questions: What key projects did Fred Rocba have access to?
What was stolen from the system?
Where was the data transferred to?
How was the data stolen (USB, cloud, network)?
When exactly did the unauthorized activity occur?
notes: This is a FOR500 (Windows Forensic Analysis) training case. The scenario focuses on physical break-in IP theft, NOT remote APT compromise. Key evidence: file access timestamps during 2020-11-13 evening, USB device insertions, cloud sync activity, browser downloads, shellbags showing folder navigation, recent docs, and jump lists. Memory image (Rocba-Memory.raw) available for volatile artifact analysis.
